title: Daily Briefing — Apr 16
headline: Mega-cap tech is melting up, but sector breadth is flashing yellow — patience, react, don’t predict.
body: Right, so the big picture: Tech is on fire, with AAPL up +2.9% at $266.43 and NVDA pushing +1.2% at $198.87. Most majors and indices (SPY $699.94, QQQ $637.40) are flying well above the 20 MA, signalling risk-on momentum. VIX at 18.15, trending below its SMA20, says volatility is calming—but the Fear & Greed Index has run from 20.4 to 57.6 in a month, so retail is piling back in.

Sector rotation shows XLK (Tech) and XLC (Comm) leading, but watch XLE (Energy), XLB (Materials), and XLU (Utilities)—all weakening with elevated risk. Financials (XLF $52.17) are improving, but barely scraping their 200 MA ($52.29). If the canary rolls, we pull back exposure immediately. Staples and healthcare remain laggards, so don’t expect defensive outperformance yet.

Contrarian risk: greed is rising but hasn’t hit that extreme red line. Still, be suspicious—upside looks stretched on several names; AAPL, NVDA, and QQQ are nearly 15% above their 20 MA. If price closes below the daily 20 MA, exit longs. Retail euphoria rising means abundance mindset: don’t chase, wait for mean reversion zones (weekly 20 MA) and dark pool signals before stacking new trades.
callout_body:
AAPL: Watch for a daily close below $254.65 (20 MA) before trimming
NVDA: 20 MA support at $179.38, risk if breached
XLF: $52.29 (200 MA) = economic canary zone
SPY: $661.91 (20 MA)—major risk if daily closes below
Fear & Greed: 57.6—rising, but not extreme (no forced risk-off yet)
XLK/QQQ: Both >10% above 20 MA—don’t chase, watch for mean reversion
action_items: Set alerts for daily closes below the 20 MA on leading tech (AAPL, NVDA, QQQ, SPY) — if triggered, exit/reduce longs per rule R006.
Monitor Financials (XLF), especially $52.29—any decisive breach triggers risk-off in broader equities per rule R001.
closing_quote: If it’s in the press, it’s in the price. Patience—next trade always comes.
